title: Down 72%, Should You Buy the Dip in SoundHound AI?
author: John Ballard
description: SoundHound AI stock has plunged 72% from all-time highs despite rapid business expansion with 52% YoY revenue growth. The company's voice AI technology is gaining traction with restaurants and automakers, and its new OASYS agentic AI platform has helped a Fortune 100 company save $10 million quarterly. However, the company remains unprofitable with a $26 million adjusted net loss and faces competition from tech giants like Google. The voice AI market is projected to reach $47 billion by 2034, offering significant long-term potential for patient investors willing to tolerate volatility.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite significant stock decline and current unprofitability, the company shows strong revenue growth (52% YoY), successful real-world applications generating measurable value ($10M quarterly savings), and operates in a high-growth market projected to reach $47 billion by 2034. The pullback is presented as a potential buying opportunity for long-term investors.

title: Joby Aviation Stock Sinks on Latest News. Will the eVTOL Ever Recover Its Lost Value?
author: John Bromels
description: Joby Aviation's stock fell 20% last week following a stronger-than-expected jobs report, which raised concerns about potential Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Despite positive company-specific news including NYC eVTOL demonstration flights, the macroeconomic outlook for high-growth companies dimmed. The stock is down over 50% from its all-time high, though FAA approval could trigger a significant recovery.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 20% last week due to macroeconomic concerns about interest rate hikes. Despite positive company-specific news (NYC demonstration flights, lawsuit dismissal), the broader economic outlook for high-growth companies weakened. Stock is down 50% from all-time high and remains speculative and volatile.

title: Nebius Group vs. SoundHound AI: Which AI Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: The article compares two AI-focused stocks with different market positions: Nebius Group, a full-stack cloud infrastructure provider for AI workloads with 350% YoY revenue growth but extremely high valuation, and SoundHound AI, a conversational AI specialist with 99% revenue growth but facing competition from tech giants. The author recommends Nebius for investors believing in AI's long-term transformation, citing that size matters in the AI industry, despite its premium valuation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong 350% YoY revenue growth, $101.7M net income with 19.2% net margin, and aggressive expansion into AI infrastructure. Author recommends it for long-term AI believers, though acknowledges very high valuation (191.6x Forward P/E) and competition from larger hyperscalers.

Impressive 99% revenue growth and established customer base (12 of 15 largest banks, 4 of 5 largest automakers), but faces significant competitive threats from tech giants like Alphabet and Apple. More attractively valued (18.9x P/S) but questions remain about competitive moat in rapidly evolving AI landscape.

title: SoundHound AI's CEO Says the Company Has a "Proven Track Record When it Comes to M&A." But Do the Numbers Really Back That Up?
author: David Jagielski, Cpa
description: SoundHound AI has aggressively pursued mergers and acquisitions to grow its revenue, which has expanded from $46 million annually to $44 million quarterly. However, the company remains deeply unprofitable with operating expenses exceeding $106 million per quarter—more than twice its revenue. Despite CEO claims of a proven M&A strategy, investors remain skeptical as the stock has declined over 20% in the past year, with concerns about cash burn and ongoing dilution.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company has achieved impressive revenue growth through acquisitions, it remains deeply unprofitable with operating expenses more than double its revenue. The stock is down 20%+ over the past year, and investors are concerned about unsustainable cash burn and ongoing dilution from stock offerings. The CEO's claims of M&A success are not supported by improving financial fundamentals.

While the company has achieved impressive revenue growth through acquisitions, it remains deeply unprofitable with operating expenses more than double its revenue. title: 3 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks That Could Make You a Millionaire
author: John Bromels
description: The article highlights three AI stocks with millionaire-making potential: Nvidia, the undisputed leader in AI processors with 85% revenue growth and 139% net income growth despite its $5 trillion market cap; Alphabet, which has successfully integrated AI into its Google Search and Cloud businesses with 19% ad revenue growth and 63% Google Cloud revenue growth; and SoundHound AI, an emerging AI voice chat specialist that recently launched its OASYS platform for autonomous AI agents, though it remains unprofitable.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Exceptional growth metrics with 85% revenue growth and 139% net income growth despite megacap status; maintains undisputed industry leadership in AI processors; stock down 6% from highs presents entry opportunity.

Successfully monetized AI integration across core business; Google Search ad revenue grew 19% to $60.4B; Google Cloud revenue surged 63% to $20B driven by enterprise AI solutions; maintains dominant market position.

Successfully monetized AI integration across core business; Google Search ad revenue grew 19% to $60.4B; Google Cloud revenue surged 63% to $20B driven by enterprise AI solutions; maintains dominant market position.

Innovative OASYS platform uses self-improving autonomous AI agents similar to AlphaGo's approach; expanding beyond core restaurant and automotive customers; revenue growing despite current unprofitability; stock down 66% from highs offers potential upside.
